reviews.title: Great sound for your buck
reviews.text: Slim design. Not a lot of weight to it which is good yet doesn't feel cheap and fragile. Very easy to connect. I was impressed with the sound quality, came in crisp and clear. Bluetooth is strong. Place it it about 10-15ft away and clear. Took calls and was able to hear them and they were able to hear me, clearly. so far, been using it on and off for the last 3 weeks and finally charged it. I can't speak if it's actually 24hour playtime, but based on this so far, it seems like the claims are true and can go on for 24 hours.

I didn’t expect much due to its relatively small size (not much bigger than a soda can) But it’s very impressive because it has big, deep sound, doesn’t distort at full volume, and battery life is excellent. Fast charging and it’s small size means it goes everywhere in my backpack.  You’d expect something this nicely built to be 10x more costly. I’ve recommended this thing to several friends

reviews.title: little beast!
reviews.text: I had this for a few months and never used it. Opened it tonight while I was going to exercise and was blown away. Can't believe the sound that comes out of this small device. I love the soft touch finish on the device (although it collects fingerprints). I used it at full volume for my entire workout and no sign of running out of battery yet. Satisfying bass, rumbles my desk. Quality sound, I occasionally heard a note or effect in the songs that I don't notice on my headphones. I did use it with an Aux cable so far so I'm not sure about BT performance.

Doesn't quite have the clarity of some other speakers I've heard but has acceptable sound quality and volume for my purposes as a cheap travel speaker or for working in the yard. I would invest in something much better if I was using it for entertaining or as my primary speaker.

reviews.author: ezflyer13

reviews.title: Great portable speaker
reviews.text: We now own two of these speakers.  One is used at work to supplement the poor performance of my laptop speakers and the second one is used around the house.  We've used the one located at our house for music in the bathroom, music while washing the cars and while grilling out.  My 18 month old has gotten ahold of it a few times and dropped if from a few feet.  No issues and the rubberized exterior holds up well to drops and use.  Battery life for us is more than acceptable.  We have to recharge it maybe once a month with our limited use.  The one I keep at work I leave connected to a USB port on my docking station so that it is constantly charged.  We picked these up for less than $20 each and have been satisfied.
reviews.author: loyaltysbp

reviews.title: Very Awesome Portable Speaker!
reviews.text: I bought this when it was on sale (what a steal), and I can say it was definitely worth the retail price. The bluetooth sync is super easy, and the sound is very crisp. Highly recommended if you're looking for a great speaker on a budget!
reviews.author: sd3lta

reviews.title: Shower jams
reviews.text: Purchased for use in the shower. I thoroughly dry and air out after each use. Sound isn't muffled, but it's decent with the barrage of a shower coming down on it. Not a long-term purchase. Pleased with its performance so far. Battery isn't too shabby for its intended use in our house.

reviews.title: Enjoyed it for a short time .
reviews.text: When I noticed Best Buy was selling it online for a bargain price I thought what a deal. A month and half later it quit working and of course according to the Best Buy website you have to return an item within thirty days for exchange or refund. I'm glad I didn't pay full price for it. I will definitely not buy another one.
reviews.author: Soob

reviews.title: Fantastic with great sound!

reviews.author: Suevona

reviews.title: Decent sounding and good volume
reviews.text: I don't particularly find any BT speaker to sound great.  Their limited driver size and range just limits what they can do.  But amongst BT speakers, the Anker holds it own against pricier and more well known offerings like JBL and Sony.  I also have a couple of Insignia BT speakers of similar size.  The Anker has a more balanced sound and can reach slightly higher volume than the Insiginia (which also sound pretty decent).  Compared to JBL's, max volume is lower, but I think it has a more balanced sound.  Of these brands, Sony still sounds best with the most well balanced response and has the best bass output, but the JBL and Sony cost much more.  For the money, the Anker is a good buy.
reviews.author: Nikke

reviews.title: Absolutely love Anker products
reviews.text: Absolutely love Anker products. They seem to be made well and durable.
